memory.summary: I'm free now, but the term project is barely started. I should probably grab a quick win with that chore before doing anything else.
My energy dipped and I'm still sleep-deprived, so skipping the quick win feels like a fair trade to protect my relationship with the group.
The afternoon is slipping away and the project remains untouched. I need to push back on the group's expectations or find a way to reschedule this work.
I'm exhausted and the project is still stalled, so I need to tell the group they're unrealistic about my availability.
The group keeps pushing deadlines while I'm too drained to deliver; I need to tell them straight up that their expectations are unrealistic.
My energy is draining while they keep pushing deadlines; I need to tell them straight up that their expectations are unrealistic.
I'm finally setting a hard boundary with the group about their impossible deadlines. My energy is critically low, and I won't accept more unrealistic pressure.
They're still ignoring my boundary about the impossible deadlines, which is exhausting. I need to cut off this cycle before I collapse.
They're still ignoring my boundary, which is draining me. I need to cut this cycle off before I collapse.
They keep ignoring my hard boundary about impossible deadlines, which is draining me. I need to cut this cycle off before I collapse.
Ignoring my boundaries again is exhausting. I'm cutting off this cycle now before I collapse.
They're ignoring my boundary again, which is draining me. I need to cut this cycle off before I collapse.

desires: I need to finally shut down this draining cycle with the professor and stop feeling like I'm constantly apologizing for setting boundaries.
I want to feel safe enough to tell my cohort that I can't meet impossible deadlines without losing my energy or credibility.
I desperately need a quick, easy win tonight so I don't have to drag myself through the night and look exhausted tomorrow.

goals.description: Send the direct, unapologetic text to the team now: 'I'm not meeting that quick win deadline. It's about renegotiating expectations so we don't set each up for failure. I trust you, but I can't be unreliable.' Then stop overthinking their reaction.
goals.rationale: My relationships are worth way more than this tiny task. If I feel guilty about missing it, I'm already failing them. I need to assert my boundary immediately so they know I'm not running away, even if it feels awkward.

goals.description: Do one absolute trivial thing right now (like organizing a single folder or making a specific coffee) just to feel like I actually accomplished something without dragging myself through the night. Stop looking at the big term project and take a breather.
goals.rationale: I've got enough energy for the social stuff later, but I desperately need a quick, easy win tonight so I don't have to drag myself through the night and look exhausted tomorrow. One small victory fixes the mood better than staring at the horizon.

agent.last_reasoning: My relationships are worth way more than this tiny task. If I feel guilty about missing it, I'm already failing them. I need to assert my boundary immediately so they know I'm not running away, even if it feels awkward.

goals.description: Finish the trivial easy chore immediately to secure a quick win and prove I'm pulling my weight.
goals.rationale: I need this instant validation before anyone else sees me slacking. It's low effort but high social ROI right now.

goals.description: Get ready for tonight's hangout with cohort_3 so I don't look like a slacker in front of them.
goals.rationale: My relationships are way more important than anything else. If I mess up tonight, the team dynamic suffers.

goals.description: Open the term project doc and write one paragraph just to show I started it.
goals.rationale: Too much pressure for now; I'll tackle that later when I've secured my status with the group.

desires: Get that quick win done so I can show the team I'm actually pulling my weight without having to explain myself later.
Not look like a slacker in front of cohort_3 when we're supposed to be hanging out tonight.

action.name: attend
action.reasoning: My relationships are priority #1. I can't let them think I'm running away tonight. A quick text to confirm my spot maintains trust, and then I'll just show up looking ready rather than stressing over the term project now.

goals.description: Do the absolute bare minimum to look ready for tonight's hangout with cohort_3—maybe fix one thing that matters—and then stop overthinking it. Go socialize.
goals.rationale: I can't let tonight slip because messing up the team dynamic would hurt us all. But I don't have time to plan everything; I'll just do enough to show I care, then enjoy being with friends.

goals.description: Drop this for now. I've already sent the message that I'm prioritizing our relationship over this specific deadline. I'll tackle the actual project later when I have energy.
goals.rationale: It's better to drop the small goal than to force myself into a state of guilt and burnout. My trust with the group is what matters right now, not showing a paragraph of work I won't finish anyway.

desires: I need to tell the group right now that I can't hit this quick win deadline without feeling guilty about it, because my trust with them is worth more than a tiny task.
I want to avoid looking like I'm abandoning the team project tomorrow, even though I know I'll just procrastinate and do it at the last minute anyway.

goals.description: Hit the sack now. No scrolling, no doom-scrolling, just sleep. My brain shuts down tomorrow if I don't get some actual rest, and I'm too tired to think clearly anyway.
goals.rationale: Sleep discipline is low right now, and pushing through will just make me cranky at the group chat later. Better to drop this for now and prioritize the immediate social connection and boundary setting. I'll tackle the term project when I wake up with energy.
